title: galium mollugo subsp. asperifolium
intro: Galium mollugo subsp. asperifolium, also known as the rough-leaved sweet woodruff, is a subspecies of perennial herb that belongs to the bedstraw family (Rubiaceae). Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which make it effective in treating a range of health issues, including digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant has been found to have a range of health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular choice in traditional medicine. Galium mollugo subsp. asperifolium can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, allowing for its medicinal properties to be easily incorporated into a healthy lifestyle.
